name: libnamespace-autoclean-perl
title: libnamespace-autoclean-perl
summary: module to remove imported symbols after compilation
description:
module to remove imported symbols after compilation
namespace::autoclean is a Perl pragma that keeps your namespace clean by
removing all imported symbols at the end of the current package's compile
cycle. Perhaps most importantly, this means that imported functions will no
longer be made accidentally accessible to users outside of your package via
method invocations.
.
This module differs from namespace::clean (see libnamespace-clean-perl) in
that it cleans all imported functions, whether or not the imports happened
prior to using the pragma. Things that "look" like a method (according to
Class::MOP) will be left alone.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libnamespace-autoclean-perl because module to remove imported symbols after compilation.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libnamespace-autoclean-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libnamespace-autoclean-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libnamespace-autoclean-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
